items.headline: Netskope Reports Q1 FY2027 Results: ARR Up 29% to $845M, Revenue Up 28% to $202M
items.whyItMatters: Netskope's quarterly report demonstrates continued hypergrowth post-IPO with ARR approaching $1B, validating the company's platform execution and SASE market positioning. ARR growth deceleration from 34% to 29% YoY is the key investor watchpoint.

items.headline: Netskope Posts First Post-IPO Earnings; Full-Year FY2026 Guidance of $701-703M Beats Street Estimates
items.whyItMatters: Beating analyst consensus of ~$690M on its inaugural public earnings report signals management credibility; the guidance raise also reflects enterprise security spending momentum driven by AI-related threat surface expansion.

items.headline: Netskope Climbs 21% After $908M IPO on NASDAQ Under Ticker NTSK
items.whyItMatters: The largest cybersecurity IPO of 2025 signaled robust institutional demand for SASE infrastructure; Lightspeed's 19.3% stake appreciated to ~$1.5B windfall. First-day close of $22.49 vs. $19 IPO price; stock has since fallen below $19.

items.headline: Netskope Shares Rise 18% in Market Debut, Yielding Lightspeed $1 Billion Return
items.whyItMatters: Lightspeed's $1B+ windfall reinforces the value creation in enterprise SASE/SSE and is likely to attract further institutional capital to the category; shares closed at $22.49 vs. $19 IPO price.

items.headline: Netskope Prices IPO at $19 Per Share, Top of Marketed Range After 20x Oversubscription
items.whyItMatters: Pricing at the top of the raised range after upsizing from $813M to $908M reflects exceptional deal quality; the book was reportedly ~20x covered, rare for software in the current rate environment.

items.headline: Netskope IPO In Demand as AI Era Heightens Cyber Threats
items.whyItMatters: Bloomberg investor demand coverage explicitly links AI adoption to expanded cloud security attack surfaces, positioning Netskope's SASE/SSE suite as a direct beneficiary of enterprise AI security budget increases.

items.headline: Netskope Files to Raise $813M in IPO; H1 Revenue Grew 31% to $328.5M
items.whyItMatters: The IPO prospectus revealed 31% first-half revenue growth and narrowing net losses ($169.5M vs. $206.7M), giving public investors their first look at one of the largest private cybersecurity companies.

items.headline: Netskope Follows Rubrik as Rare Cybersecurity IPO; Both Backed by Lightspeed
items.whyItMatters: TechCrunch contextualizes Netskope as only the second major cybersecurity IPO of 2025, noting Lightspeed's 19.3% ownership and back-to-back wins cementing it as the defining SASE/security franchise investor.

items.headline: Netskope Files for IPO Showing Revenue Rise; Last Private Valuation $7.5B in 2021
items.whyItMatters: The S-1 filing confirmed Netskope's revenue trajectory and put a formal IPO process in motion after years of speculation; the 2021 private mark of $7.5B sets the baseline for IPO price discovery.

items.headline: Netskope Raises $300M Series H at $7.5B Valuation, Led by ICONIQ Growth
items.whyItMatters: The Series H at $7.5B cemented Netskope as one of the most valuable private cybersecurity companies globally; the round provided runway to scale enterprise sales and build out the NewEdge network ahead of an eventual public offering.
